unit: Unit 8: Special Equations and Classical Models
title: Special Equations and Classical Models
subtitle: Singular solutions, classical curve problems, and the modeling tradition from fluids to ecology and economics.
description: Singular solutions, classical curve problems, and the modeling tradition from fluids to ecology and economics.

atAGlance.title: Unit 8 at a Glance: Core Sub-Topics
atAGlance.summary: Unit 8 moves from the algebraic theory of first-order ODEs into the classical applied models that drive physics, biology, and economics. It opens with two \emph{special equation types}---the Clairaut equation (singular-solution envelopes) and pursuit/tractrix curves---before building three major applied models: the Navier--Stokes-derived plane Poiseuille velocity profile, the supply-and-demand price-adjustment ODE, and the Lotka--Volterra predator-prey and competition systems. The unit closes with a structured survey of first-order solution methods.
bullets: \textbf{8.1 The Clairaut Equation and Singular Solutions} --- general solution $y = Cx + f(C)$; singular (envelope) solution from the system $\{y = xp + f(p),\; 0 = x + f'(p)\}$.
\textbf{8.2 Pursuit Curves and the Tractrix} --- tractrix DE $dy/dx = -\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}/x$; general pursuit $x\,y'' = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}$ with speed ratio $k = v_m/v_d$.
\textbf{8.3 Fluid Dynamics and Viscosity} --- Newtonian shear $\tau = \mu\,du/dy$; plane Poiseuille flow $\mu u'' = -G$ with no-slip boundary conditions.
\textbf{8.4 Economic Models, Supply, Demand, and Price} --- price ODE $dp/dt = k(D - S)$; equilibrium $p^* = (a+c)/(b+d)$; convergence for $k(b+d) > 0$.
\textbf{8.5 The Lotka--Volterra Predator-Prey Model} --- coexistence equilibrium $(c/d,\, a/b)$; conserved quantity $H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$.
\textbf{8.6 Competition Models} --- two-species carrying-capacity system; nullcline inequalities for stable coexistence versus competitive exclusion.
\textbf{8.7 A Survey of Solution Methods} --- decision tree: separable, linear (integrating factor), Bernoulli, exact; classify by structure before solving.

cheatSubtitle: Condensed Cheat Sheet: Special Equations and Classical Models

cheatTakeaway.title: Unit 8 Key Takeaway
cheatTakeaway.body:
Every Clairaut equation $y = xy' + f(y')$ yields a
\textcolor{accentorange}{general line family $y = Cx + f(C)$} and a
\textcolor{accentorange}{singular envelope} obtained by eliminating $p$ from
$x + f'(p) = 0$. Pursuit geometry converts tangency and speed constraints
into separable second-order ODEs. The Poiseuille profile
\textcolor{accentorange}{$u = (G/2\mu)\,y(h-y)$} and the price path
\textcolor{accentorange}{$p(t) = p^* + (p_0 - p^*)e^{-k(b+d)t}$} both follow
from linear BVPs. In Lotka--Volterra dynamics the
\textcolor{accentorange}{conserved quantity $H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$}
organizes all phase-plane orbits as closed curves around the coexistence
equilibrium $(c/d, a/b)$; in two-species competition the entire outcome is
decided by \textcolor{accentorange}{$\alpha\beta < 1$ (stable coexistence) versus
$\alpha\beta > 1$ (competitive exclusion)}.

modules.title: The Clairaut Equation and Singular Solutions
modules.topics: A \emph{Clairaut equation} has the special form $y = xy' + f(y')$. Setting $p = y'$ and differentiating both sides with respect to $x$ yields $(x + f'(p))\,dp/dx = 0$, splitting into two branches whose solutions are the general family of straight lines and the singular envelope.

formulas.title: Clairaut Equation: General and Singular Solutions
formulas.body:
For $y = xp + f(p)$ with $p = y'$:
\[
  \textbf{General solution:}\quad y = Cx + f(C) \quad (p = C = \text{const}),
\]
\[
  \textbf{Singular solution (envelope):}\quad
  \begin{cases}
    y = xp + f(p) \\
    0 = x + f'(p)
  \end{cases}
\]
Eliminate $p$ from this system to obtain the envelope: a curve tangent to every
line in the general family.
The singular solution satisfies the ODE but \emph{cannot} be obtained from the
general solution by any choice of $C$.

methodology.title: Envelope Elimination Procedure
steps: Write the equation as $F(x,y,C) = y - Cx - f(C) = 0$.
Differentiate with respect to $C$: $\partial F/\partial C = -x - f'(C) = 0$, giving $x = -f'(C)$.
Solve for $C = C(x)$ and substitute back into $F = 0$ to obtain the envelope curve $y = y(x)$.
Verify: the envelope must satisfy the original ODE directly.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Singular Solution Is Not a Particular Solution

modules.title: Pursuit Curves and the Tractrix
modules.topics: A \emph{pursuit curve} traces the path of a pursuer who always aims directly at a moving target. The tractrix is the classical limiting case in which the pursuer is dragged at constant distance $a$ from a target walking along a line.

formulas.title: Tractrix Differential Equation and Solution
formulas.body:
Target on the $y$-axis, pursuer starts at $(a,0)$, equal speeds, string length $a$:
\[
  \frac{dy}{dx} = -\frac{\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}}{x}, \qquad 0 < x \le a,\quad y(a) = 0.
\]
Solution (via $x = a\sin\theta$):
\[
  y(x) = a\ln\!\left(\frac{a + \sqrt{a^2 - x^2}}{x}\right) - \sqrt{a^2 - x^2}.
\]

formulas.title: General Pursuit Equation
formulas.body:
Dog at origin pursues owner on line $x = c$ at speed $v_m$; dog speed $v_d > v_m$;
$k = v_m/v_d < 1$:
\[
  x\,y'' = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}, \qquad y(c) = 0,\quad y'(c) = 0.
\]
Solution ($k \ne 1$):
\[
  y(x) = \frac{1}{2}\!\left[\frac{x^{k+1}}{(k+1)c^k}
  - \frac{c^k x^{1-k}}{1-k}\right] + \frac{ck}{1-k^2}.
\]
Capture height: $y(0) = ck/(1-k^2)$.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Direction of the Derivative Sign
warning.body:
In the tractrix, $dy/dx$ is \emph{negative} because $x$ decreases as the pursuer
is dragged toward the $y$-axis. Always verify the geometric sign from the
tangent-segment picture before writing the ODE.

modules.title: Fluid Dynamics and Viscosity
modules.topics: Newtonian viscosity: $\tau = \mu\,du/dy$. Plane Poiseuille flow reduces the momentum balance to a second-order constant-coefficient ODE with a parabolic solution.

formulas.title: Plane Poiseuille Velocity Profile
formulas.body:
$\mu\,u''(y) = -G$, $u(0) = u(h) = 0$:
\[
  u(y) = \frac{G}{2\mu}\,y(h - y), \qquad
  y_{\max} = \frac{h}{2}, \qquad
  u_{\max} = \frac{Gh^2}{8\mu}.
\]
Shear stress: $\tau(y) = \tfrac{G}{2}(h - 2y)$, linear, zero at the centerline.

formulas.title: 2D Incompressible Continuity and Stream Function
formulas.body:
$\partial u/\partial x + \partial v/\partial y = 0$.
Given $u$: integrate $\partial v/\partial y = -\partial u/\partial x$ in $y$.
Stream function: $u = \partial\psi/\partial y$, $v = -\partial\psi/\partial x$;
streamlines are $\psi(x,y) = C$.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Both No-Slip Conditions Are Required
warning.body:
The Poiseuille ODE produces a quadratic with two free constants. Both
$u(0) = 0$ and $u(h) = 0$ must be applied; using only one gives a wrong family.

modules.title: Economic Models, Supply, Demand, and Price
modules.topics: Price adjusts at a rate proportional to excess demand. With linear demand and supply the ODE is first-order linear, and the equilibrium price is globally stable.

formulas.title: Price Adjustment ODE and Equilibrium
formulas.body:
$D = a - bp$, $S = -c + dp$ ($a,b,c,d > 0$):
\[
  \frac{dp}{dt} = k\bigl[(a+c) - (b+d)p\bigr],
  \qquad
  p(t) = p^* + (p_0 - p^*)\,e^{-k(b+d)t},
  \qquad
  p^* = \frac{a+c}{b+d}.
\]
Convergence: $k(b+d) > 0$ always.

methodology.title: Solving the Price ODE via Integrating Factor

modules.title: The Lotka--Volterra Predator-Prey Model
modules.topics: The Lotka--Volterra system produces closed phase-plane orbits guaranteed by the first integral $H(x,y)$.

formulas.title: Lotka--Volterra System and Conserved Quantity
formulas.body:
$\dot{x} = ax - bxy$, $\dot{y} = -cy + dxy$ ($a,b,c,d > 0$):
\[
  E_0 = (0,0), \qquad E^* = (c/d,\; a/b).
\]
\[
  H(x,y) = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y = \text{const} \quad
  (\text{minimum at } E^*).
\]

methodology.title: Deriving the Conserved Quantity
steps: Rearrange: $H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$.
Verify $\nabla H = 0$ at $E^*$ and Hessian positive definite.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: The Coexistence Equilibrium Is a Center
warning.body:
$(c/d, a/b)$ is a \emph{center} (closed orbits), not a stable spiral.
Perturbations do not damp out in the pure Lotka--Volterra model; adding
saturation converts the center to a stable spiral.

modules.title: Competition Models
modules.topics: Two-species competition: the outcome depends on the relative positions of the $x$- and $y$-nullclines.

formulas.title: Competition System and Interior Equilibrium
formulas.body:
$\dot{x} = r_1 x(1 - (x + \alpha y)/K_1)$,\;
$\dot{y} = r_2 y(1 - (y + \beta x)/K_2)$.
\[
  E_3 = (x^*, y^*), \quad
  x^* = \frac{K_1 - \alpha K_2}{1 - \alpha\beta}, \quad
  y^* = \frac{K_2 - \beta K_1}{1 - \alpha\beta}.
\]
Nullclines: $x + \alpha y = K_1$ and $\beta x + y = K_2$.

methodology.title: Coexistence vs.\ Exclusion: Nullcline Intercept Test

concept.title: Unit 8 Key Takeaway --- Part I
concept.body:
Clairaut equations split into a \textcolor{accentorange}{general line family
$y = Cx + f(C)$} and a \textcolor{accentorange}{singular envelope} from
$x + f'(p) = 0$. The Poiseuille profile
\textcolor{accentorange}{$u = (G/2\mu)\,y(h-y)$} and price path
\textcolor{accentorange}{$p(t) = p^* + (p_0 - p^*)e^{-k(b+d)t}$} follow from
linear BVPs. Lotka--Volterra orbits are level sets of
\textcolor{accentorange}{$H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$}; competition outcomes
follow from \textcolor{accentorange}{$\alpha\beta < 1$} (coexistence) versus
\textcolor{accentorange}{$\alpha\beta > 1$} (exclusion).

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Interior Equilibrium Feasibility
warning.body:
$E_3$ is biologically meaningful only when both $x^* > 0$ and $y^* > 0$.
Check the signs of numerators and denominator before drawing conclusions.

practice.subtitle: Practice Set: Problems and Complete Solutions
practice.intro: Work the following ten problems in order. Problems 1--2 develop the Clairaut equation: general straight-line family and singular envelope elimination (Module 8.1). Problems 3--4 derive and solve the tractrix ODE and the general pursuit equation with a speed-ratio parameter (Module 8.2). Problem 5 solves the plane Poiseuille velocity profile with no-slip boundary conditions, and Problem 6 applies 2D incompressible continuity to find the stream function (Module 8.3). Problem 7 solves the linear price-adjustment ODE and identifies the equilibrium (Module 8.4). Problem 8 analyzes the full Lotka--Volterra predator-prey system including the conserved quantity $H$ (Module 8.5). Problem 9 classifies coexistence versus competitive exclusion in a two-species competition model via nullcline inequalities (Module 8.6). Problem 10 closes with a four-part classification and solution survey (Module 8.7).
problems: Find the general solution and the singular solution of the Clairaut equation $y = xy' + (y')^2$. Verify that the singular solution is the envelope of the general straight-line family.

For the Clairaut equation $y = xy' - \ln(y')$ (with $y' > 0$), find the one-parameter family of straight-line solutions and the singular solution. Confirm that the singular solution satisfies the ODE directly.

A target moves from the origin up the positive $y$-axis. A pursuer starts at $(a,0)$ and is always connected to the target by a taut string of length $a$, with both moving at the same speed. Derive the tractrix differential equation \[ \frac{dy}{dx} = -\frac{\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}}{x}, \qquad y(a) = 0, \] from the constant-length tangent-line geometry, then solve it for $y(x)$ using the substitution $x = a\sin\theta$.

A dog at the origin always runs directly toward its owner, who walks along $x = c$ at speed $v_m$. The dog runs at speed $v_d > v_m$ with $k = v_m/v_d$. Derive $x\,y'' = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}$, $y(c) = 0$, $y'(c) = 0$. Set $p = y'$, solve the separable equation in $p$, integrate to find $y(x)$, and state the finite capture height $y(0)$.
Solve $\mu\,u''(y) = -G$, $u(0) = u(h) = 0$ for the velocity profile $u(y)$. State the location and value of the maximum velocity.

Given $u = x^2 - y^2$ for a 2D incompressible steady flow, find the most general $v(x,y)$ consistent with $\partial u/\partial x + \partial v/\partial y = 0$. For $g(x) = 0$, determine the stream function $\psi(x,y)$ and write the streamline equation.
Price obeys $dp/dt = k(D - S)$ with $D = a - bp$, $S = -c + dp$ ($a,b,c,d,k > 0$). Write the ODE, solve via integrating factor with $p(0) = p_0$, find $p^*$, and state the convergence condition.

For $\dot{x} = ax - bxy$, $\dot{y} = -cy + dxy$ ($a,b,c,d > 0$): interpret each parameter; find both equilibria; derive the conserved quantity $H(x,y) = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$; verify $H$ is minimized at $(c/d, a/b)$.

For the competition system with parameters $r_1, r_2, K_1, K_2, \alpha, \beta$: find all four equilibria; write the nullclines; state the inequalities governing stable coexistence, bistable exclusion, and single-species dominance; derive $(x^*, y^*)$ explicitly.
Classify and solve completely: (a) $dy/dx = x(1+y^2)/(1+x^2)$;\; (b) $dy/dx + (2/x)y = x^3$;\; (c) $dy/dx + y = xy^3$;\; (d) $(2xy + \cos x)\,dx + (x^2 - \sin y)\,dy = 0$.
solutions:
\noindent\textbf{Solution 1.}\quad
Set $p = y'$. The Clairaut equation is $y = xp + p^2$.

Differentiate both sides with respect to $x$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{p = p + x\,\frac{dp}{dx} + 2p\,\frac{dp}{dx}
  = p + (x + 2p)\,\frac{dp}{dx}.}
\]
Cancel $p$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{(x + 2p)\,\frac{dp}{dx} = 0.}
\]

\emph{Case A:} $dp/dx = 0 \Rightarrow p = C$.
\textbf{General solution:} $y = Cx + C^2$.

\emph{Case B:} $x + 2p = 0 \Rightarrow p = -x/2$. Substitute:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{y = x\!\left(-\frac{x}{2}\right)
  + \left(-\frac{x}{2}\right)^2
  = -\frac{x^2}{2} + \frac{x^2}{4} = -\frac{x^2}{4}.}
\]

Envelope check: $F = y - Cx - C^2$; $\partial F/\partial C = -x - 2C = 0$,
so $C = -x/2$. Substituting:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{y + \frac{x^2}{2} - \frac{x^2}{4}
  = y + \frac{x^2}{4} = 0 \;\Longrightarrow\; y = -\frac{x^2}{4}.}
\]
Direct check: $y' = -x/2$, $(y')^2 = x^2/4$,
$xy' + (y')^2 = -x^2/2 + x^2/4 = -x^2/4 = y$. \checkmark

\noindent\textbf{Solution 2.}\quad
Set $p = y' > 0$. The equation is $y = xp - \ln p$.

Differentiate:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{p = p + \!\left(x - \frac{1}{p}\right)\frac{dp}{dx}
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \left(x - \frac{1}{p}\right)\frac{dp}{dx} = 0.}
\]

\emph{Case A:} $p = C$.
\textbf{General solution:} $y = Cx - \ln C$.

\emph{Case B:} $p = 1/x$. Substitute:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{y = x\cdot\frac{1}{x} - \ln\!\frac{1}{x} = 1 + \ln x.}
\]
\textbf{Singular solution:} $y = 1 + \ln x$.

Envelope: $F = y - Cx + \ln C$; $\partial F/\partial C = -x + 1/C = 0$,
so $C = 1/x$. Then $y - 1 - \ln x = 0$. \checkmark

Direct check: $y' = 1/x$; $xy' - \ln y' = 1 + \ln x = y$. \checkmark

\noindent\textbf{Solution 3.}\quad
At $(x,y)$ the string has horizontal leg $x$ and hypotenuse $a$, so vertical
leg $= \sqrt{a^2 - x^2}$. Since $x$ decreases as the pursuer is dragged:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{\frac{dy}{dx} = -\frac{\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}}{x}.}
\]

Substitute $x = a\sin\theta$, $dx = a\cos\theta\,d\theta$,
$\sqrt{a^2 - x^2} = a\cos\theta$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{\int\frac{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}{x}\,dx
  = a\int\frac{\cos^2\theta}{\sin\theta}\,d\theta
  = a\int(\csc\theta - \sin\theta)\,d\theta
  = a\bigl[\ln|\csc\theta - \cot\theta| + \cos\theta\bigr].}
\]
Back-substitute ($\csc\theta = a/x$, $\cot\theta = \sqrt{a^2-x^2}/x$,
$\cos\theta = \sqrt{a^2-x^2}/a$):
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{= a\ln\!\left(\frac{a - \sqrt{a^2-x^2}}{x}\right)
  + \sqrt{a^2-x^2}.}
\]
So $y = -a\ln\!\bigl((a-\sqrt{a^2-x^2})/x\bigr) - \sqrt{a^2-x^2} + K$.
Applying $y(a) = 0$ gives $K = 0$. Rationalizing:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{y(x) = a\ln\!\left(\frac{a + \sqrt{a^2-x^2}}{x}\right)
  - \sqrt{a^2-x^2}.}
\]

\noindent\textbf{Solution 4.}\quad
Tangency condition at dog position $(x,y)$ with owner at $(c,Y)$:
$Y - y = (c-x)y'$. Differentiate in $x$: $dY/dx = (c-x)y''$.
Speed ratio: $dY/dx = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}$.
Equating: $(c-x)y'' = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}$, i.e.\ $xy'' = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}$
(after shifting).

Let $p = y'$; separate $dp/\sqrt{1+p^2} = k\,dx/x$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{\operatorname{arcsinh}(p) = k\ln x + C_1
  \;\Longrightarrow\;
  p + \sqrt{1+p^2} = A\,x^k.}
\]
$y'(c) = 0$: $A = c^{-k}$. Using $(\sqrt{1+p^2}+p)(\sqrt{1+p^2}-p) = 1$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{2p = (x/c)^k - (x/c)^{-k}
  \;\Longrightarrow\;
  y' = \frac{1}{2}\!\left[(x/c)^k - (x/c)^{-k}\right].}
\]
Integrate ($k \ne 1$):
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{y = \frac{1}{2}\!\left[\frac{x^{k+1}}{(k+1)c^k}
  - \frac{c^k x^{1-k}}{1-k}\right] + C_2.}
\]
$y(c) = 0$: $C_2 = ck/(1-k^2)$.

\textbf{Solution:}
$y(x) = \tfrac{1}{2}\bigl[x^{k+1}/((k+1)c^k) - c^k x^{1-k}/(1-k)\bigr] + ck/(1-k^2)$.
Capture height: $\textcolor{workpurple}{y(0) = ck/(1-k^2)}$.

\noindent\textbf{Solution 5.}\quad
Integrate $\mu u'' = -G$ twice:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{\mu u'(y) = -Gy + C_1,
  \qquad
  \mu u(y) = -\frac{G}{2}y^2 + C_1 y + C_2.}
\]
$u(0) = 0 \Rightarrow C_2 = 0$.\quad
$u(h) = 0 \Rightarrow C_1 = Gh/2$.
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{u(y) = \frac{G}{2\mu}\,y(h-y).}
\]
$u'(y) = (G/2\mu)(h-2y) = 0$ at $y = h/2$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{u_{\max} = \frac{G}{2\mu}\cdot\frac{h}{2}\cdot\frac{h}{2}
  = \frac{Gh^2}{8\mu}.}
\]

\noindent\textbf{Solution 6.}\quad
$\partial u/\partial x = 2x$, so $\partial v/\partial y = -2x$.
Integrate in $y$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{v(x,y) = -2xy + g(x).}
\]
With $g = 0$, find $\psi$ from $\partial\psi/\partial y = x^2 - y^2$:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{\psi = x^2 y - \frac{y^3}{3} + h(x).}
\]
Check $-\partial\psi/\partial x = -2xy - h'(x) = v = -2xy$,
so $h' = 0$, $h = \text{const}$. Streamlines:
\[
  \textcolor{workpurple}{x^2 y - \frac{y^3}{3} = k.}
\]

takeaway.title: Unit 8 Key Takeaway
takeaway.body:
Every Clairaut equation $y = xy' + f(y')$ yields a
\textcolor{accentorange}{general line family $y = Cx + f(C)$} and a
\textcolor{accentorange}{singular envelope} obtained by eliminating $p$ from
$x + f'(p) = 0$. Pursuit geometry converts tangency and speed constraints
into separable second-order ODEs. The Poiseuille profile
\textcolor{accentorange}{$u = (G/2\mu)\,y(h-y)$} and the price path
\textcolor{accentorange}{$p(t) = p^* + (p_0 - p^*)e^{-k(b+d)t}$} both follow
from linear BVPs. In Lotka--Volterra dynamics the
\textcolor{accentorange}{conserved quantity $H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$}
organizes all phase-plane orbits as closed curves around the coexistence
equilibrium $(c/d, a/b)$; in two-species competition the entire outcome is
decided by \textcolor{accentorange}{$\alpha\beta < 1$ (stable coexistence) versus
$\alpha\beta > 1$ (competitive exclusion)}.
